change: Added Williams %R(14) < -80 alongside existing Market cap > $2B and RSI(14) < 30, requiring two independent oversold signals to agree. Reduced matches 36->32. Does not fix the CRWD-style false positive (short-term dip inside an uptrend still clears both oscillators) - step 2's 52-week-high distance check remains mandatory for that. Stochastic Oscillator filter attempted and abandoned - scanner backend has a template bug on FILTER_TYPE_STOCHASTIC_OSCILLATOR regardless of parameters.

Tightened PEG<1.5->0.35, ROE>15%->25%, added Operating margin>0 and Net profit margin>0 (previously no profitability filter at all). Reduced matches 394->71. Direct application of the Quality Minus Junk profitability/quality logic (RUBRICS.md citation) enforced at the scan level instead of only in step 4's manual read.

Added Net profit margin > 0 (user-directed: only focus on stocks already earning positive profit). Reduced matches 32->20. Tradeoff flagged explicitly: HOOD and INTC were not reliably profitable at their actual beaten-down entry points per validation.md, so this could exclude a future HOOD/INTC-style setup in exchange for screening out higher-risk still-unprofitable distressed names. Deliberate user choice, not an oversight - revisit via rubric-engine once enough turnaround rows resolve.

Added Net profit margin > 0 (same user-directed profitability requirement, applied consistently across all four archetypes). Reduced matches 76->61. Side benefit: this scan previously had zero fundamental columns at all, so this also makes Category B (net margin) computable directly from the scan output for the first time.

Added Net profit margin > 0 (same user-directed profitability requirement). Matches ~91->~94 (roughly flat - Operating margin>10% already implied positive profit for nearly every match in this archetype). Now gives a real Net profit margin column for Category B instead of relying on Operating margin as a proxy.
